POS; ORDER GRANTNG EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR AN ORDER SHORTENING TIME ON A MTN TO VACATE JUDGMENT AND SET ASIDE JUDGENT FILED BY DEFENDANT LYSSIAK, ALEXANDRE VASILIVICH
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-25-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF ROBERT NAVARRO'S MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO STIPULATION IS GRANTED. THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT DEFENDANT WAS GIVEN NOTICE OF THE EX-PARTE HEARING TO ENFORCE THE TERMS OF THE STIPULATED JUDGMENT. THERE IS NOTHING WRONG WITH THE STIPULATED JUDGMENT AND NO BASIS FOR THE COURT TO VACATE OR RESCIND IT. PLAINTIFF DID MAKE ARRANGEMENT TO INSTALL TELEPHONE LINES AS SUPPORTED BY THE DECLARATION OF PLAINTIFF AND AT&T BILLS WITHIN 3 WEEKS FROM THE AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. PER DIEM FEES ASSESSED BY THE COURT. ORDER AND JUDGMENT WERE SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189
